message:
fix(h2/frame): correct RFC 7540 error classification for PRIORITY and WINDOW_UPDATE

PRIORITY frame (Section 6.3): A PRIORITY frame with wrong length is a
stream-level error, not a connection error. Changed parse to return
H2Error::stream with FrameSizeError code instead of H2Error::frame_size.
Updated existing test assertion to verify stream_id is Some(1).

WINDOW_UPDATE frame (Section 6.9): A zero-increment WINDOW_UPDATE on a
specific stream (stream_id != 0) is a stream error, not a connection
error. On stream 0 it remains a connection error. Split the error
return into a conditional: stream_id==0 returns connection-level
PROTOCOL_ERROR, non-zero stream_id returns stream-level PROTOCOL_ERROR
with the stream_id attached. Updated existing zero-increment test to
verify stream_id is None (connection error) and added new
test_window_update_zero_increment_stream_level_is_stream_error test
verifying stream_id is Some(3) for stream-level rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
